usage_notes: Universal moisturizer. Safe for all skin types and ages.
Use on damp skin to avoid drawing moisture from dermis. Multiple MW versions beneficial.
Smaller MW than HA; penetrates deeper into skin layers.
Can irritate sensitive skin at higher concentrations. Patch test recommended.
Gentler than propylene glycol. Good for sensitive skin.
Can be mildly comedogenic at high concentrations in occlusive formulas.
Pro-Vitamin B5. Excellent wound healing and barrier support.
Low % (2-10%) moisturizing; high % (20-40%) keratolytic. Avoid broken skin.
Naturally found in skin. Excellent NMF component.
Derived from sugar beet. Excellent for sensitive/rosacea skin.
Protects skin proteins during environmental stress.
Non-comedogenic. Excellent for all skin types including acne-prone.
Technically a wax ester. Mimics sebum. Good for oily/acne skin.
High in linoleic acid. May not suit acne-prone skin. Check individual tolerance.
Rich in Vitamin E and fatty acids. Moderate comedogenicity.
Avoid with nut allergies. Comedogenicity rating 2.
Highly comedogenic (4). Best used on body, not face for acne-prone.
Comedogenicity 0. High linoleic acid. Excellent for acne and sensitive skin.
Unrefined may cause breakouts. Refined shea better for acne-prone.
Non-comedogenic silicone. Great primer/base ingredient. Safe for sensitive skin.
Fatty alcohol, not drying alcohol. Non-irritating.
Comedogenicity 3. Avoid in acne-prone skin. Penetration enhancer.
Well-refined mineral oil safe; heavy application may increase acne risk.
Best occlusives known. Not comedogenic per research but may trap sebum.
Low pH required (2.5-3.5). Unstable; use fresh. Avoid mixing with niacinamide simultaneously.
Stable form of Vitamin C. Works at higher pH. Good for sensitive skin.
Converts to ascorbic acid in skin. Stable and gentle.
Very stable. Oil and water compatible. Effective brightener.

avoid_combining_with: AHAs/BHAs (avoid same time)|benzoyl peroxide|vitamin C (avoid same time)
usage_notes: START low and slow. Use PM only. Always SPF. Not for pregnancy. Purging possible. Avoid with other actives until skin adapted.
Gentler retinoid. Less effective than retinol but more tolerable.
11x more potent than retinol. Gentler than tretinoin. PM only. Start slow.
Next-gen retinoid. Gentler than retinol with comparable efficacy. Better for sensitive.
Safe retinol alternative. Can use AM/PM. Pregnancy safe. Can combine with retinoids.
Multitasking ingredient. 5-10% optimal. May cause flushing at very high %%.
Effective brightener. More stable than beta arbutin. Pregnancy caution due to hydroquinone parent.
Can be irritating. Use with caution on sensitive skin. Limit to 2%.
Very well tolerated. Excellent for PIH and melasma.
Safe in pregnancy at OTC %. Excellent for rosacea and PIH.

avoid_combining_with: retinol (same time)|vitamin C (same time)|BHA (same time at high %)
usage_notes: Smallest AHA molecule, deepest penetration. Start 5-10%. Always SPF. Not in pregnancy.
Gentler than glycolic. Moisturizing properties. Better for dry/sensitive. Some prefer in pregnancy.
Largest AHA molecule, gentlest. Good for acne-prone and sensitive skin.

avoid_combining_with: retinol (same time at high %)|vitamin C (low pH)
usage_notes: Oil-soluble, penetrates pores. Best for acne/oily skin. Avoid in pregnancy. Can be drying.
Gentlest exfoliant. Antioxidant. Excellent for sensitive skin. Safe in pregnancy.
Best for sensitive skin. Anti-inflammatory. Non-comedogenic. Safe in pregnancy.
Physical UV filter. Very safe. May leave white cast. Not irritating.
Unstable alone; needs photostabilizer. Avoid in pregnancy.
Photostabilizes avobenzone. Can cause contact dermatitis in some.
Photostable. Excellent broad-spectrum. Not approved in US.
Comedogenicity 2. Pure vitamin E. Synergistic with Vitamin C.
Esterified form. More stable but must convert in skin. Less potent than pure tocopherol.
Pairs well with Vitamin C. Photounstable; use PM.
Decreases with age. Good for energy-depleted mature skin.
Dramatically stabilizes Vitamins C and E. Best in combination.
Matrixyl. Effective at very low concentrations. Very well tolerated.
Argireline. Topical muscle relaxer. Best at expression lines.
GHK-Cu. Do not use same session as pure vitamin C or retinoids.
Essential for skin barrier. Works best with cholesterol and fatty acids.
Works synergistically with other ceramides.
Excellent for acne-prone and compromised barrier skin.
Critical skin barrier lipid. Forms ideal ratio with ceramides and fatty acids.
Excellent for sensitive and compromised skin. Wound healing.
Derived from chamomile. Excellent for inflamed/sensitive skin.
Cica. Excellent for compromised barrier, eczema, rosacea, post-procedure.
Base of many soothing products. Excellent first-aid ingredient.
Colloidal oat is FDA approved for eczema. Excellent for sensitive skin.
Glabridin is key brightening component. Good for melasma.
EGCG is primary active. Good for acne and anti-aging.
Very effective for P. acnes. Oxidizing agent. Bleaches fabric. Not for sensitive/dry.
Always dilute. Can cause contact dermatitis. Effective against P. acnes.
Gentle antimicrobial. Good for mild-moderate acne. Some odor.
Use alcohol-free version for sensitive skin. Can be drying.
FDA approved for anti-wrinkle claims. Very well tolerated.
Good for eye area puffiness and dark circles. Antioxidant.
Powerful antioxidant. Good in PM routine.
Holds 5000x its weight in water. More effective than HA.
Generally safe. Some reports of sensitivity in babies. EU limit 1%.
Multi-functional. Gentle. Supports phenoxyethanol.
Universal formula base. The principal solvent in cosmetics. No skin concerns at typical use levels.
Sugar alcohol humectant. Very gentle and well tolerated. Stabilises formulas against temperature changes.
Vitamin B8-like compound. Gentle moisturising and skin brightening support.
Prebiotic sugar. Selectively nourishes beneficial skin bacteria and provides mild moisturising.
Birch-derived sugar alcohol. Humectant and mild antimicrobial. Supports skin microbiome balance.

skin_concerns_worsens: acne (pure form - more comedogenic than squalane)
usage_notes: Precursor to squalane. Naturally secreted by sebaceous glands. More comedogenic than squalane. Squalane preferred in formulation.
Rich in oleic acid and tocopherols. Antioxidant and quickly absorbing. Comedogenicity 3.
Very high carotenoid and vitamin C content. Use diluted (1-5%) to avoid orange staining of skin and fabrics. Exceptionally nutrient-dense.
Rich in omega-3/6/9 fatty acids. Light texture. Suitable for dry and mature skin.
Traditional Japanese tsubaki oil. High in oleic acid and polyphenols. Absorbs rapidly without greasiness.
Rich and emollient. High oleic acid and sterols. Best for dry and mature skin. Comedogenicity 3.
High linoleic acid content. Lightweight. Non-comedogenic. Clinically shown to preserve skin barrier in neonates.
May weaken skin barrier in neonates and eczema-prone skin at high concentrations. Best for dry/mature adults.
Rosa canina seed oil. High linoleic acid and beta-carotene. Trans-retinoic acid precursor supports anti-aging. May trigger breakouts in some.
Fractionated coconut oil. Very lightweight and non-greasy ester. Excellent carrier oil for actives. Suitable for all skin types.
C18 fatty alcohol. Non-drying and non-irritating. Thickens emulsions and adds creamy texture.
Volatile cyclic silicone. Evaporates leaving smooth skin feel. Growing environmental persistence concerns (D4/D5 restrictions in EU).
C16-C18 fatty alcohol blend. Excellent emulsion stabiliser and skin conditioner. Well tolerated.
Sheep wool-derived wax. Highly effective emollient and occlusive. Avoid if wool-allergic. May cause contact dermatitis in sensitised individuals.
Comedogenicity 4. Best used on body. Rich in stearic acid and polyphenols. Antioxidant properties.
Lighter feel than shea butter. Good emollient for dry skin. Comedogenicity 2.
Natural wax providing occlusive barrier and texture. Zero to low comedogenicity. May trigger reactions in rare bee-product allergies.
Hard plant wax from Brazilian carnauba palm. Non-comedogenic. Used in lip products and solid formulas for structure and gloss.
Oil-soluble stable Vitamin C ester. Works at neutral pH. Gentle and highly stable. Penetrates skin effectively.
Hydroquinone-beta-D-glucoside. More common and affordable than alpha arbutin. Slightly less potent. Pregnancy caution due to hydroquinone parent compound.
Oil-soluble kojic acid ester. More stable and gentler than free kojic acid. Effective brightener at 1-3%.
Grape-derived AHA. Gentler than glycolic acid. Often combined with other AHAs in exfoliant formulas.
Primarily a pH adjuster at low concentrations. Mild AHA and chelating agent. Antioxidant synergist.
Apple-derived AHA. Gentler than glycolic. Often blended with other AHAs. Good for mild exfoliation.
Gentler BHA alternative to salicylic acid. Suitable for sensitive acne-prone skin. Lower irritancy than salicylic acid.
Broad term covering gluconolactone and lactobionic acid. Very gentle multifunctional exfoliant. Safe for sensitive skin.
Galactose-gluconic acid PHA. Most moisturising PHA. Chelating and antioxidant properties. Excellent for rosacea-prone skin.
IP6 from rice/corn. Chelates metal ions triggering oxidation. Gentle tyrosinase inhibitor and antioxidant.
Alpha-lipoic acid. Unique water- and oil-soluble antioxidant. Can cause sensitisation at high concentrations. Use at 0.1-0.5%.
Matrixyl 3000 component (with Palmitoyl Tetrapeptide-7). Stimulates pro-collagen I. Very well tolerated.
Matrixyl 3000 component. Reduces IL-6 inflammatory cascade. Anti-aging and anti-inflammatory peptide.
Acetyl Glutamyl Heptapeptide-3. Synergises strongly with Argireline (Acetyl Hexapeptide-3) for enhanced anti-wrinkle effect.
Acetyl Octapeptide-3. Extended version of Argireline. Targets expression lines by inhibiting SNARE complex.
Dipeptide Diaminobutyroyl Benzylamide Diacetate. Synthetic waglerin-1 analog. Relaxes facial muscles. Use in PM formulas.
Acetyl Glutamyl Heptapeptide-1. Complements Argireline in anti-wrinkle formulas. Use in combination for best results.
Common UVB filter (Ethylhexyl Methoxycinnamate). Estrogenic activity concerns. Not recommended during pregnancy. Photostable.
Benzophenone-3. Broad UVA/UVB coverage. Endocrine disruption concerns and coral reef toxicity. Prohibited in several regions. Avoid in pregnancy.
Common UVB filter. Mild endocrine disruption concerns. Not recommended in pregnancy. Photostable.
Ethylhexyl Salicylate. UVB filter. Also improves avobenzone photostability. Low sensitisation potential.
Methylene Bis-Benzotriazolyl MBBT. Micro-particulate broad-spectrum filter. Photostable. Not approved in US/Canada.
Ecamsule. Superior UVA coverage. Not FDA approved in US. Well tolerated. Found in La Roche-Posay products.
Drometrizole Trisiloxane. L'Oreal proprietary broad-spectrum filter. Synergises with Mexoryl SX for full UVA coverage.
Multifunctional preservative booster and skin conditioner. Often paired with phenoxyethanol. Very well tolerated.

avoid_combining_with: ascorbic acid (low pH may form benzene)
usage_notes: Effective antimicrobial at pH below 5. Caution: combines with Vitamin C at low pH under heat to form trace benzene. Widely used and considered safe at regulated levels.
Natural antifungal preservative. Active at acidic pH. Gentle. Often combined with sodium benzoate for broad preservation.
Most used paraben preservative. Considered safe by EU CosIng and FDA at regulated concentrations. Avoid if paraben-sensitive.
EU max 0.14% in leave-on. Mild endocrine disruption concern at high doses. Precautionary avoidance in pregnancy recommended.
Contact allergen in fragrance-sensitive individuals. Also a natural preservative. Avoid on broken or very sensitive skin.
Broad-spectrum antimicrobial. EU limit 0.3% in leave-on. Not recommended for products applied to children under 3 years.
Effective antifungal at acidic pH. Often combined with benzyl alcohol. Very mild irritancy profile.
Natural preservative active only at pH below 5. Mild sensitiser in some individuals. Use at lowest effective concentration.
Natural antifungal acid. Active at acidic pH. E200 food-grade preservative. Very gentle profile.
Gentle glycerol monostearate co-emulsifier. Also mildly conditions skin. One of the safest emulsifiers available.
Sugar-based non-ionic emulsifier. Natural origin. Very gentle. Excellent for sensitive and baby skin formulas.
Highly effective anionic surfactant. Can significantly disrupt skin barrier with repeated use. Avoid in dry/sensitive/eczema skin. Rinse-off use only.
Also known as Capriol 90. Dual-function humectant and preservative booster. Mild sensitisation risk at concentrations above 1%. Very effective at low levels.
Ethoxylated SLS. Milder due to larger molecule. May contain trace 1
Coconut-derived amphoteric surfactant. Milder than anionic surfactants. Can cause contact allergy. Rinse-off use.
Mild non-ionic emulsifier. Solubilises fragrance and essential oils in water-based formulas. Very gentle.
PEG-60 sorbitan ester. Thicker emulsifier than Polysorbate 20. Used in creams and body lotions. Mild.
PEG-80 sorbitan ester. Slightly comedogenic (1). Can mildly enhance the penetration of certain actives.
Phosphatidylcholine from soy or egg. Supports skin barrier lipid structure. May be problematic for severe soy allergy.
Butylated hydroxytoluene. Synthetic antioxidant. Stabilises formulas. Used at trace levels. Considered safe at regulated concentrations.
Butylated hydroxyanisole. Antioxidant preservative at very low percentages. Safety within cosmetic use limits is well established.
CoQ10 synthetic analog. More stable and bioavailable than CoQ10. Powerful antioxidant for anti-aging formulas.
Centella asiatica triterpene glucoside. Key cica active. Stimulates collagen synthesis and repairs compromised barrier.
Centella glycoside. Promotes wound healing and collagen production. Excellent for post-procedure and compromised skin.
Ceramide 1. Contains linoleic acid esterified at omega position. Critical for lamellar body function. Works synergistically with all other ceramides and cholesterol.
Vitamin B5 free acid. Soothing and wound healing. Precursor to Panthenol in skin metabolism.
Rich in bisabolol and apigenin. Anti-inflammatory. Excellent for sensitive and reactive skin. Avoid if allergic to Asteraceae family.
Pot marigold. Triterpenoid-rich wound-healing botanical. Avoid if allergic to Asteraceae/Compositae family.
High water content and mild astringent properties. Cooling effect. Excellent for puffy and irritated skin.
sh-Oligopeptide-1. Stimulates epidermal cell proliferation. Effective at extremely low concentrations. Stability sensitive to pH and heat.
Collagen peptides. Film-forming on skin surface. Moisturising and conditioning. Cannot replace structural dermal collagen topically.
Keratin peptides. Film-forming and barrier strengthening. Better documented for hair applications but beneficial for skin barrier too.
Silk amino acids from sericin/fibroin. Imparts silky texture. Film-forming. Conditioning and moisturising.

skin_concerns_worsens: irritation at high concentration or undiluted
usage_notes: Caustic soda. Used in trace amounts only to adjust formulation pH. Safe at typical cosmetic concentrations.
Used primarily as pH adjuster at low concentrations. Chelating agent. Distinguished from AHA exfoliant use by concentration.
TEA. Neutralises carbomers and acidic actives. Can cause sensitisation. EU max 2.5% in leave-on. Do not use with nitrosating agents.
Chelates heavy metals to prevent formulation rancidity and enhance preservative efficacy. Standard safe cosmetic ingredient.
Tetrasodium salt of EDTA. More water soluble than disodium form. Penetration enhancement concern noted at high percentages.
Polyacrylic acid cross-polymer (e.g. Carbopol 940). Must be neutralised (NaOH or TEA) to develop viscosity. Creates clear stable gels.
Bacterial fermentation polysaccharide. Excellent rheology modifier. Slightly moisturising. Very safe and widely used.
HPMC. Semi-synthetic cellulose derivative. Film-forming and thickening. Very safe and inert.
HEC. Water-soluble cellulose ether. Gentle thickener for transparent gels. Very safe.
Acrylamide polymer thickener. The polymer is safe; the residual acrylamide monomer is regulated to very low levels. Used in Sepigel 305.
Super-absorbent acrylate polymer. Creates extremely smooth skin feel. Excellent for oily skin mattifying formulas.
Red algae polysaccharide. Natural gelling agent. Vegan gelatin alternative. Very safe.
Red seaweed polysaccharide. Undegraded carrageenan considered safe in cosmetics. Provides slip and stability.
Fragrance blend. Most common contact allergen in cosmetics. Avoid in sensitive and reactive skin products. EU requires labelling of 26 allergenic fragrance components.
Soothing and antimicrobial. Contains linalool and linalyl acetate. Oxidised linalool can cause sensitisation in some users.
Rosa damascena extract. Gentle antioxidant and soothing properties. Suitable for all skin types including sensitive.
Curcumin is primary active. Potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory. Can stain skin and fabrics yellow. Use at low concentrations.
Azadirachta indica. Contains nimbidin and nimbin. Potent antimicrobial. Strong natural odour. Dilute appropriately.
Marine algae polysaccharide extract. Moisturising and antioxidant. Suitable for all skin types.
Very high ORAC antioxidant value. Ellagic acid and punicalagins support brightening and anti-aging.
White clay. Oil-absorbing and mild cleansing. Can be drying with overuse. Not suitable as primary ingredient for dry or sensitive skin.
Montmorillonite clay. Highly absorbent. Very drying with overuse. Best for oily skin masks. Rinse-off use.
Zinc salt with astringent and antimicrobial properties. Used in toners and acne treatments to reduce sebum and bacteria.
Silicon dioxide. Mattifies and absorbs oil. Provides slip and soft-focus optical effect. Non-irritating.
Dimethylaminoethanol. Proposed firming mechanism debated. Can temporarily swell cells. Use at 0.5-2%. Not for pregnancy.
Biopeptide EL. Palmitoyl-GHK. Stimulates collagen and hyaluronic acid synthesis. Very well tolerated.
High molecular weight hyaluronic acid. Forms film on skin surface for immediate moisture. Complements low MW Sodium Hyaluronate.
Sodium salt of gluconic acid. Gentle chelating agent and mild humectant. Very safe and well tolerated.
Sodium chloride from sea. Physical exfoliant in scrubs. Can sting on broken or sensitive skin. Rinse-off use only.
Contains glycoproteins/mucopolysaccharides/glycolic acid/zinc/copper. Multi-benefit ingredient. Higher % used in dedicated snail mucin serums.
Oat and yeast-derived polysaccharide. Activates skin innate immune response. Superb moisturiser and soothing agent for sensitive/mature skin.
Natural extremolyte from halophilic bacteria. Stabilises skin proteins and DNA against environmental stress. Exceptional for sensitive skin.
Yeast ferment Pitera. Rich in vitamins/minerals/amino acids. Multibenefit brightening and pore-refining. Key ingredient in SK-II products.
Probiotic-derived lysate. Strengthens skin microbiome diversity and barrier function. Excellent for sensitive and compromised skin.
Yeast ferment filtrate. Brightening and moisturising. Contains vitamins B and amino acids. Synergises well with niacinamide.
Bee-derived resinous substance. Potent antimicrobial and antioxidant. Avoid if allergic to bees or bee products.
Korean mugwort (Ssuk). Rich in flavonoids and monoterpenes. Excellent for eczema-prone and sensitive skin barrier repair.
Contains salicin (natural BHA precursor). Gentler exfoliation than synthetic salicylic acid. Antioxidant polyphenols. Good for acne-prone skin.
MAP. Stable water-soluble Vitamin C phosphate. Works at higher pH than ascorbic acid. Very gentle and suitable for sensitive skin.
Potent but gentle tyrosinase inhibitor. Effective at 0.3-1%. Low irritancy compared to kojic acid or hydroquinone.
4-Hexylresorcinol. Potent brightener and antimicrobial. Sensitisation possible at high concentrations. Use at sub-1%.
Ketocarotenoid from marine microalgae. 6000x antioxidant potency vs Vitamin C. Can impart reddish colour to formula at higher concentrations.
Naturally occurring thio-histidine betaine. Transporter-mediated uptake into skin cells. Protects mitochondrial DNA. Very gentle.
Master cellular antioxidant. Topical bioavailability debated; encapsulated or oxidised forms may improve delivery. Brightening at higher topical concentrations.
Ceramide 2. N-Stearoylsphingosine. Works synergistically with Ceramide NP and Ceramide AP to restore barrier lipid matrix.
Ceramide 5. Alpha-hydroxy fatty acid ceramide. Part of the complete ceramide complex for full barrier repair.
Ceramide 4. N-Stearoyldihydrosphingosine. Dihydroceramide that rounds out the ceramide complex formulation.
Beta-sitosterol/stigmasterol from plants. Mimic cholesterol in the skin barrier. Anti-inflammatory. Good complement to ceramide formulas.
Saturated C18 fatty acid. Gives creams body and opaque appearance. Comedogenicity 3. Naturally occurs in skin lipids.
Omega-9 fatty acid. Penetration enhancer. Comedogenicity 3. Predominant in olive oil. Good for dry and mature skin.
Omega-6 essential fatty acid. Deficient in acne-prone sebum. Anti-comedogenic and barrier-repairing. Brightening via inhibition of tyrosinase.
Saturated C16 fatty acid. Occurs naturally in human skin lipids. Slightly comedogenic at high concentrations. Comedogenicity 2.
Very high linoleic acid content. Lightweight non-greasy texture. Low comedogenicity 1. Good for oily and acne-prone skin.
Limnanthes alba oil. Extremely oxidatively stable (long shelf life). Non-greasy. Suitable for all skin types.
Black seed oil. Contains thymoquinone with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Pronounced natural smell. Use diluted at 5-20%.
Very high ALA omega-3 content. Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant. Lightweight. Good for sensitive and mature skin.
Opuntia ficus-indica seed oil. Among the highest Vitamin E content of any cosmetic oil. Premium anti-aging antioxidant.
Pentavitin. Carbohydrate complex that chemically bonds to keratin proteins for sustained 72-hour hydration. Mimics skin NMF.
NMF component. Sodium salt of lactic acid. Excellent humectant. Also mild exfoliant when formulated at lower pH. Very well tolerated.
Val-Trp valinyl-tryptophan. Eyeseryl. Reduces under-eye puffiness by stimulating lymphatic drainage. Best in eye area products.
Dermican. Stimulates lumican and collagen I synthesis. Firming anti-aging peptide. Excellent for formulas targeting skin laxity.
Gly-Pro-Hyp collagen sequence tripeptide. Directly stimulates Type I collagen synthesis. Very well tolerated.
Thymulen 4. Mimics thymopoietin signalling. Promotes skin renewal and firmness. Anti-aging peptide.
Melanostatine 5. Inhibits alpha-MSH receptor to reduce melanin production. Effective brightening peptide at low concentrations.
Sepiwhite MSH. Phenylalanine derivative that competes with alpha-MSH for receptor binding. Inhibits melanin synthesis pathway.
Isobutylamido Thiazolyl Resorcinol. Eucerin-patented ingredient. One of the most effective topical tyrosinase inhibitors. Excellent clinical data for melasma.
Sym White 377 active (1-12-dodecanedioic acid). Safe dicarboxylic acid brightener. Well tolerated with consistent pigmentation results.
Mixed alpha/beta arbutin. Hydroquinone glucoside. Widely used brightener. Pregnancy caution due to hydroquinone parent. Effective tyrosinase inhibitor.
Pomegranate-derived polyphenol. Tyrosinase inhibitor and antioxidant. Clinically effective brightener.
Broad antimicrobial; effective against Malassezia yeast linked to dandruff and pityrosporum folliculitis. Useful for fungal acne treatment.
Octopirox. Antifungal and antibacterial. Effective for seborrheic dermatitis and Malassezia-associated skin conditions.
Potent keratolytic and tyrosinase inhibitor. Irritating at higher percentages. Not for sensitive or dry skin. Avoid in pregnancy.
PHSA. Used in anhydrous and W/O emulsions. Excellent for dispersing mineral pigments in sunscreen formulas.
Branched C18 fatty acid. Non-crystallising at room temperature. Excellent for lip products and anhydrous formulas.
D6 cyclic silicone. Larger ring size than D5. Similar function to cyclomethicone. Environmental bioaccumulation concerns parallel those of other cyclic silicones.
Coconut fatty acid isethionate salt. Exceptionally mild. Used in solid bars and cream cleansers. Leaves skin feeling conditioned.
C12-alkyl glucoside. Sugar-based mild surfactant from natural origin. Gentle enough for sensitive skin. Used in rinse-off formulas.
C10-alkyl glucoside. Gentlest of the alkyl glucoside surfactants. Suitable for baby and hypoallergenic formulas. Rinse-off use.
Ester emollient. Non-greasy. Good for lightweight moisturisers and make-up formulas.
Hydrogenated phospholipid. More oxidatively stable than regular lecithin. Supports liposomal delivery and barrier reinforcement.
Snow mushroom polysaccharide. Holds up to 500x its weight in water. Comparable to hyaluronic acid in moisturising efficacy. Very gentle.
Ganoderma lucidum. Beta-glucans and triterpenes. Adaptogenic anti-inflammatory. Anti-aging for stressed mature skin.
Inonotus obliquus. One of the highest ORAC-value botanicals. Contains melanin-like compounds and betulinic acid. Powerful antioxidant.
Brown seaweed Laminaria digitata. Rich in fucoidans and iodine. Anti-aging and detoxifying properties.
Bladderwrack seaweed. High iodine and fucoidan content. Antioxidant and slimming properties. Avoid if iodine-sensitive or with thyroid conditions.
DMI. Significantly enhances penetration of other actives (e.g. Vitamin C/AHAs). Use judiciously with potent actives on sensitive skin.
MSM. Bioavailable organic sulfur compound. Anti-inflammatory. May support keratin and collagen integrity. Well tolerated.
Bioavailable zinc salt. Anti-inflammatory and sebostatic. Reduces P. acnes and sebum production. Good for oily/acne skin.
1-Methylnicotinamide (MNA). More stable niacinamide derivative. Sirtuin-activating anti-aging properties. Less risk of niacin flush.
Genistein/daidzein from soy. Mild phytoestrogenic effect. Anti-aging and brightening relevant for perimenopausal skin. Pregnancy consult.
Retinal. 11x more potent than retinol. Converts directly to retinoic acid. PM use only. Always SPF. Not in pregnancy. Start slow.
Granactive Retinoid. HPR. Direct retinoic acid receptor agonist ester. No enzymatic conversion required. Gentler than retinol. Works at neutral pH.
Carnosic acid and rosmarinal primary antioxidants. Also used as natural preservative booster. Can be irritating to sensitive skin at higher concentrations.
Low MW HA fragments. Penetrates deeper into epidermis than high MW HA. Effective anti-aging ingredient. Complement with HMW HA for full-depth hydration.
Biopeptide CL. Palmitoyl-VGVAPG. Stimulates elastin and fibronectin synthesis. Complements collagen-stimulating peptides.
Elastin peptides. Surface film-forming and moisturising. Supports skin elasticity appearance. Well tolerated.
Ricinoleic acid-rich vegetable oil. Non-comedogenic despite thick viscosity. Common in lip products. Comedogenicity 1.
Flaxseed oil. Very high ALA omega-3. Oxidises rapidly - use with antioxidant. Comedogenicity 4. Best for dry/mature skin.
High GLA gamma-linolenic acid content. Clinically beneficial for atopic dermatitis. Excellent for dry/sensitive/eczema and hormonal skin concerns.
Calophyllum inophyllum. Contains calophyllolide and xanthones. Excellent for post-acne scars and keloids. Start low if sensitive.
High-linoleic safflower oil is very lightweight and non-comedogenic. Comedogenicity 0. Good for acne-prone and oily skin.
High Vitamin E and omega-3/6. Claimed natural SPF is disputed - not a replacement for sunscreen. Excellent antioxidant emollient.
Unique punicic acid omega-5 conjugated linolenic acid. Promotes keratinocyte proliferation. Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant.
Behenic acid-rich Moringa oleifera oil. Exceptional oxidative stability. Excellent for urban/anti-pollution skincare formulas.
Amphoteric coconut-derived surfactant. Builds viscosity and conditioning. Excellent for baby and sensitive skin cleansers.
Miranol-type amphoteric. Anti-static properties. Gentle cleansing. Good for sensitive skin and low-irritation formulas.
Symsave H. Natural-origin preservative booster from 4-hydroxyacetophenone. Very mild. Effective against gram-positive bacteria and fungi.
PEG-20 Cetearyl Ether. Efficient O/W emulsifier. May contain trace 1
BTMS. Cationic emulsifier. Produces light stable emulsions. Excellent skin and hair conditioning and anti-static properties.
W/O emulsifier. Used in anhydrous and water-in-oil formulas. Very gentle. Good for sensitive skin anhydrous formulas.
Naturally-derived coconut/corn emulsifier. Skin conditioning. Suitable for natural and sensitive skin formulations. Biodegradable.
Monopotassium phosphate KH2PO4. Maintains product pH and formula stability. Common buffer component in pharmaceutical/cosmetic systems.
Dipotassium hydrogen phosphate K2HPO4. Dibasic phosphate buffer. Paired with Potassium Phosphate to create complete phosphate buffer system.
Use daily with sunscreen; effective for dark spots and uneven tone.
Lightweight wax esters that soften skin without heavy occlusion.
Calming oat extract helps reduce irritation and supports compromised barrier.
Dermatology-supported soothing protectant for eczema-prone and reactive skin.
Licorice flavonoids can reduce visible discoloration and post-inflammatory marks.
Azelaic derivative with better tolerance for oily and sensitive-acne skin.
Often paired with niacinamide to support tone-evening and barrier recovery.
Peptide signal ingredient; avoid layering with low-pH acid peels in same routine.
Supports skin barrier and can reduce irritation in minimalist routines.
Long-lasting humectant hydration with low tack and good tolerance.
Use alternate nights when combined with other exfoliating actives.
LHA can be potent; introduce slowly and avoid same-night stacking with retinoids.
Licorice-derived soothing salt useful in anti-redness formulations.
Oil-soluble vitamin C derivative with improved stability and tolerability.
Eye-area peptide active; pair with moisturizers and sunscreen.
Use in peptide-focused routines and separate from low-pH peel products.
Signal peptide often paired with tetrapeptides in anti-aging serums.
Helps defend against glycation-related signs of aging.
Hydration-supportive humectant that layers well with HA and glycerin.
Part of a sugar humectant trio to increase hydration reservoirs in the stratum corneum.
Humectant sugar derivative frequently combined with xylitol derivatives.
